title: Companies are increasingly favoring temporary staff over permanent hires

description: A cutting-edge robotic system developed by Sony has demonstrated human-level competitive ability in table tennis, raising new questions about how artificial intelligence can operate in fast-moving, unpredictable environments.
content:

title: Nobel prize-winning physicist warns humanity has only ‘35 years left’
description: A leading Nobel Prize-winning scientist has raised concerns over the accelerating risks posed by nuclear weapons and artificial intelligence, suggesting humanity faces a narrowing window to avoid global catastrophe.
content:

title: It's All Things AI & You at TiEcon 2026 as the Countdown Begins
description: TiEcon 2026, themed "AI & You: Human Centered, AI Powered," will convene Silicon Valley's elite from April 29 to May 1 at the Santa Clara Convention Center. Featuring Nobel Laureates and industry leaders, the event will explore eight key tracks, including Applied AI, Cybersecurity, and AI Infrastructure, focusing on the human-centric future of artificial intelligence.

title: Banks not getting expected benefits from AI due to outdated systems: McKinsey
description: While banks are championing artificial intelligence with hefty investments, the anticipated benefits are falling short. According to a McKinsey report, legacy systems are stifling AI innovation. The core problem is the tendency to tack AI onto existing modes of operation rather than reinventing them.

title: Paging Dr. AI to the ER? Artificial intelligence shows promise in emergency room diagnosis
description: As hospitals increase their use of artificial intelligence to improve patient care, a U.S. study has found that an AI tool has proven effective at making emergency medical diagnoses — even better than actual doctors, in some cases.
